root_text.sn48.169-178:1.5: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ uddhambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya pañcindriyāni bhāvetabbāni.
root_text.sn48.169-178:1.7: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddhindriyaṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ. Vīriyindriyaṁ …pe… satindriyaṁ … samādhindriyaṁ … paññindriyaṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn48.169-178:1.8: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ uddhambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya imāni pañcindriyāni bhāvetabbānī”ti.

translation_text.sn48.169-178:1.3: Desire for rebirth in the realm of luminous form, desire for rebirth in the formless realm, conceit, restlessness, and ignorance.
translation_text.sn48.169-178:1.4: These are the five higher fetters.
translation_text.sn48.169-178:1.5: The five faculties should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these five higher fetters.
translation_text.sn48.169-178:1.7: It’s when a mendicant develops the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.sn48.169-178:1.8: These five faculties should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these five higher fetters.”
translation_text.sn48.169-178:2.4: aggregates, and fetters high and low.
translation_text.sn48.169-178:2.5: The Linked Discourses on the Faculties is the fourth section.
